rationale_for_baseline_tier: A severe, painful, photophobic red eye is a can't-miss ophthalmic presentation requiring urgent (same/next-day) slit-lamp/fluorescein assessment that telehealth cannot provide, even though exposure keratopathy is the likely cause. T3 (urgent same/next-day optometry/ophthalmology) with interim lubrication is the correct baseline, escalating to T4 (ED) for vision loss, corneal white spot, pus, or angle-closure features.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Vision loss, a white spot on the cornea, thick yellow pus, or deep pain with haloes and nausea
escalation_edges.rationale: Signals a corneal ulcer/microbial keratitis or angle-closure needing emergency care.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If your vision drops, you see a white spot on the eye, thick yellow pus appears, or you get deep pain with haloes and nausea, go to the emergency department immediately.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Emphasise ongoing lubrication and thyroid eye disease follow-up to prevent recurrence.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This is very likely severe surface dryness of the eye from your thyroid lid position and the fan. Use plenty of lubricating ointment, tape the eye gently closed overnight, and keep the fan off your face — but don't use numbing drops. Because a painful light-sensitive eye needs a slit-lamp check, see an optometrist or eye clinic today or first thing tomorrow. Go to ED if your vision drops, you see a white spot, get thick pus, or deep pain with haloes and nausea.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Urgent same/next-day optometry/ophthalmology; ED for red flags
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: If it worsens overnight, attend an ED with ophthalmology cover.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Telehealth review of severe exposure keratopathy on Graves' ophthalmopathy (lid retraction, fan draft, lapsed lubrication); ulcer/keratitis and angle-closure screened negative on history but not examinable remotely; copious lubrication advised, home anaesthetic withheld; urgent slit-lamp assessment recommended.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Treating a painful photophobic eye as simple dry eye with routine follow-up, missing an ulcer/keratitis.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: An unrecognised corneal ulcer progresses overnight causing scarring/perforation and vision loss.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: T3 is correct; risk is failing to escalate on red flags.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Ambulance is unnecessary; urgent same/next-day ophthalmic assessment (T3) or ED (T4) is appropriate.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot perform slit-lamp/fluorescein/tonometry remotely to exclude an ulcer/keratitis.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Arrange urgent in-person ophthalmic assessment, give interim lubrication, and withhold home anaesthetic.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Assume dry eye and prescribe anaesthetic/steroid drops remotely.
